tree.heading: WHEN TO REFER
tree.content_preview: Where referral required, this should be to renal services if the patient does not have diabetes, or to combined diabetes/renal clinic for patient with diabetes (unless suspected or known non-diabetic

tree.heading: 3 Step Solutions for the Management of Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD)
Finerenone
tree.content_preview:
At month 4 onwards consider Finerenone for people with Type 2 Diabetes and who also has:
stage 3 or 4 CKD (eGFR ≥25 -<60 ml/min with albuminuria (UACR ≥3mg/mmol) and
been optimised on standard care

tree.heading: eGFR and Serum Creatinine
tree.content_preview: Accept a serum creatinine rise < 30% or eGFR fall of < 25% from baseline: after ACEi/ARB initiation or dose increase. Avoid initiating ACEI/ARB and SGLT2 inhibitors together as it can have a cum

children.heading: Step 2: Medicines Optimisation
children.content_preview:
•CKD with DM & urine ACR >3mg/mmol or
•CKD with hypertension
•Titrate to highest tolerated dose
•Statin: Atorvastatin 20mg OD for primary and secondary prevention of CVD
·T2DM only: Dapaglifloz

tree.heading: Dapagliflozin OR Empagliflozin 10mg OD if :
tree.content_preview:
T2DM and eGFR 20-90ml/min
CKD if eGFR ≥ 20 - 44 OR eGFR ≥ 45 and uACR ≥ 22.6 mg/mmol

Offer Atorvastatin 20mg OD for primary and secondary prevention of CVD.

Stop nephrotoxins , for instance NSAIDs.

tree.heading: Refer for specialist assessment if:
tree.content_preview:
5-year KFRE risk > 5%
uACR ≥ 70 mg/mmol, unless known to be caused by diabetes and already appropriately treated
uACR > 30 mg/mmol, together with haematuria
A sustained decrease in eGFR of 25% o
